title: eriogonum niveum var. greenei
intro: Eriogonum niveum var. greenei, also known as white sagebrush buckwheat or white buckwheat sage, is a variety of flowering plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It is native to the Great Basin region of North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. Eriogonum niveum var. greenei can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its health benefits, making it a valuable resource for herbalists and natural healers.
